english: n. something that people talk or write about
example: Miss Gwen wrote a topic on the board and faced the class.

english: v. to discuss ideas as a group
v. to move something to a higher position
example: All hands were raised except Jimmy's.

english: n. a silly or nervous laugh
example: "He can do it really well," said Eric with a giggle.

english: v. to use unfair or dishonest methods to gain an advantage
example: I can learn to cheat on tests like Eric.
He concentrated on brainstorming more ideas.

english: v. to beat or strike with a loud noise
example: He seemed to have banged his head on the wall.

english: n. the inside surface at the top of a room
example: It was not the wall, it was the ceiling!

english: v. to move in the air
example: It was a ballon, it was floating.

english: v. to move something with a sharp, quick motion
example: He tried to jerk his head this way and that.
